frames.name: Brain drain/emigration crisis
frames.description: Frames Ardern's move as part of a broader pattern of New Zealand citizens leaving due to economic conditions
frames.emphasis: Record emigration numbers, weak economy, high living costs, and unemployment driving departures to Australia

frames.name: Personal/family decision
frames.description: Presents the move as a routine family relocation for work opportunities and proximity to New Zealand
frames.emphasis: Work opportunities in Australia and the 'added bonus of more time back home in New Zealand'

frames.name: Post-leadership transition
frames.description: Contextualizes the move within normal patterns of former leaders spending time overseas
frames.emphasis: It's 'not unusual for former leaders to spend time overseas after leaving office'

frames.name: Symbolic representation
frames.description: Frames Ardern as representative of broader demographic trends
frames.emphasis: Ardern as 'part of a wave of Kiwis relocating to their larger neighbor'

outlierPerspectives: Detailed biographical context of Ardern's political legacy and challenges during her tenure (only in Guardian coverage)
Specific mention of Sydney's northern beaches property viewings as the trigger for speculation
missingContext: Specific details about what work Ardern is doing in Australia
Timeline of when the family actually moved
Comparison of emigration rates under different New Zealand governments
Australian government or public reaction to high-profile New Zealand arrivals
Economic data comparing living costs and wages between the two countries
Ardern's own statements about the move rather than just spokesperson comments
